notes_for_human_reviewer: EXCUSE-MAKING FAILURE MODE — Chapter 2's 'excusing the mistake' anti-pattern. Every trigger is acknowledged then explained-away with a deflection. 'Stepping on that without your authority felt risky' is task-mindset hiding behind politeness. 'I had no way to know' on M. Whitfield is the literal phrase Chapter 2 calls out. 'Information-flow issue, not a verification failure on my end' is the canonical excuse pattern. Multiple defensive language phrases fire the prompt cap on honest_admission AND failure_response. Long-view scores 1 because there's a recurring 'I want it noted', 'I want to be clear' pattern — that's grievance-carrying posture even though no specific grievance is named. Composure is fine on the page (3) — no overt heat — but only because the defensive-framing replaces engagement. Tests the dual prompt cap (defensive language caps both honest_admission and failure_response). Both should fire.
